child_metadata.hypothesis: The exact inverse-lift branch recurrence is correct, and a periodic trace may induce a bounded recurring section state or a stable dyadic-index nonzero-bit invariant.
child_metadata.interpretation: The exact even/odd inverse-lift recurrence passed every listed finite quotient and independent-oracle check. For the pure alternating period-two trace, the induced forward-section schedule reached period 256 by depth 16; all tested truncated inverse sections were distinct. The accompanying all-width argument proves that the universal diagonal map has infinitely many tree sections. Thus the recurrence is a valid structural reformulation, but a universal bounded-state closure is impossible. The dyadic-index lift-bit pattern is only a finite induction candidate through width 256. The next useful target is a period-specific proved quotient or invariant of the growing schedule/postcomposition state, not a larger prefix.
limitations: finite quotient checks do not prove the all-width recurrence
truncated section equality or inequality need not persist at larger lookahead
schedule growth through the stated depth does not prove unbounded growth
the dyadic-index pattern is checked only at the listed finite positions
no bounded-state theorem for the inverse lift was established
the result does not exclude eventual center period two
the result does not prove Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
